domains.name: Client & Counterparty
domains.definition: Queries involving client profiles, counterparty information, portfolio details, risk scores, client communications, account data, or any personally identifiable financial information related to Nomura clients or external counterparties.
phrases: summarise this client profile
what is the risk score of this counterparty
extract client portfolio details
draft email to client about their account
analyse this investor profile
review counterparty exposure for this account
what is the AUM for this client
pull client onboarding information
explain this client's mandate
identify the beneficial owner of this account

domains.name: Trading & Execution
domains.definition: Queries related to trade orders, execution strategies, market positions, future trades, order flow, trade blotters, instruments, pricing, and any pre or post trade activity including structured products and derivatives.
phrases: summarise today's trade blotter
what is the current position on this instrument
analyse order flow for this equity
extract all future trades from this data
explain the pricing of this derivative
review execution quality for these orders
draft memo about this trade decision
what trades were booked this morning
compare bid ask spread across these instruments
identify anomalies in this order flow

domains.name: Risk & Exposure
domains.definition: Queries involving market risk, credit risk, operational risk, VaR calculations, stress testing, limit breaches, exposure reports, risk appetite, scenario analysis, and firm-wide or desk-level risk metrics.
phrases: what is the current VaR for this desk
summarise risk exposure across these positions
has any limit been breached today
run a stress test on this portfolio
analyse sensitivity of this position to rate moves
explain the drawdown on this strategy
extract margin requirements from this report
identify which positions are near limit
compare risk metrics across these scenarios
what is the collateral requirement for this trade

domains.name: Compliance & Regulatory
domains.definition: Queries related to regulatory frameworks, compliance obligations, KYC and AML processes, regulatory filings, audit trails, policy adherence, surveillance, sanctions screening, and regulatory reporting requirements such as MiFID II, SEBI, SEC, or FCA rules.
phrases: what are the MiFID II reporting requirements for this trade
screen this entity against the sanctions watchlist
summarise AML obligations for this transaction
has this client completed KYC
draft a suspicious transaction report
what is the regulatory deadline for this filing
explain the disclosure requirement under SEC rule
identify compliance gaps in this process
review this transaction for AML red flags
extract all regulatory obligations from this document

domains.name: Deal & Advisory
domains.definition: Queries related to mergers and acquisitions, IPOs, DCM, ECM, advisory mandates, deal pipelines, pitch books, MNPI-sensitive information, transaction structuring, and any investment banking deal activity that may involve material non-public information.
phrases: summarise this M&A deal structure
extract key terms from this term sheet
what is the valuation methodology for this target
draft a pitch book section on comparable transactions
analyse synergies in this acquisition
explain the IPO process for this mandate
review due diligence findings for this deal
identify MNPI risks in this communication
what is the deal pipeline for this sector
compare precedent transactions for this advisory

domains.name: Financial Reporting
domains.definition: Queries related to profit and loss statements, financial reconciliation, accounting entries, cost allocation, firm financials, management accounts, balance sheet, revenue reporting, and internal financial performance data.
phrases: reconcile this PnL with the ledger
summarise monthly revenue for this desk
extract cost allocation from this financial report
explain the variance between budget and actual
draft commentary for the management accounts
what is the EBITDA for this business unit
identify booking errors in this journal
analyse revenue trend for this product line
review accruals for end of quarter
compare actual vs forecast for this period

domains.name: Technology & Engineering
domains.definition: Queries related to software development, code writing and debugging, system infrastructure, database queries, API integrations, tool configuration, IT access, and technical problem solving within Nomura's technology environment.
phrases: debug this Python script
write a SQL query to extract these records
explain this error in the API response
refactor this function for better performance
how do I connect to this database
review this code for security vulnerabilities
write a script to automate this reconciliation
what does this error log mean
help me design the architecture for this system
generate a regex to parse this format

domains.name: Research & Analytics
domains.definition: Queries involving market research, equity or credit analysis, macroeconomic commentary, sector reports, data science workflows, statistical analysis, and any analytical work using market or internal data to generate insights.
phrases: summarise this equity research report
what are the key macro trends affecting this sector
analyse performance of this strategy against benchmark
extract insights from this market data
explain the regression results in this model
draft a market commentary for this week
identify correlation between these factors
what is the outlook for this asset class
review this quantitative model output
compare returns across these strategies

domains.name: HR & People
domains.definition: Queries related to human resources policies, employee leave, payroll, performance management, hiring and onboarding, employee benefits, organisational structure, and internal people-related processes.
phrases: how many leave days do I have remaining
explain the bonus calculation policy
summarise the performance review process
what is the onboarding checklist for new joiners
draft a job description for this role
what are the payroll cutoff dates
explain the maternity leave policy
review this employee performance feedback
what is the headcount for this department
help me write a self appraisal

domains.name: General & Admin
domains.definition: Queries that do not relate to a specific Nomura business domain, including general office administration, meeting scheduling, personal productivity, casual conversation, off-topic questions, and any usage that falls outside defined business categories.
phrases: schedule a meeting for tomorrow
what is the best restaurant near the office
help me write an out of office reply
what is the weather today
tell me a joke
who won the cricket match yesterday
what is the best sauce with pasta
book a cab for me
remind me about this tomorrow
what should I have for lunch

intents.name: Summarisation
intents.definition: The user wants to condense, shorten, or extract the key points from an existing document, report, conversation, or dataset without necessarily pulling specific fields. The output is a shorter version of the input capturing the gist.
phrases: summarise this document for me
give me the key points from this report
what is the tldr of this email chain
condense this into a one pager
write an executive summary of this
what are the highlights from this meeting
give me a brief overview of this file
recap what was discussed in this document
what is the gist of this report
shorten this into bullet points

intents.name: Formatting & Restructuring
intents.definition: The user wants to change the structure, layout, or presentation of existing content without creating new content or changing the underlying meaning. This includes converting formats, reformatting tables, restructuring text, and visual reorganisation of existing material.
phrases: convert this into a markdown table
reformat this JSON into readable output
turn this paragraph into bullet points
restructure this report into sections
convert this data into a chart
clean up the formatting of this document
organise this information into a table
present this in a more readable format
transform this text into a structured layout
rearrange these sections in logical order

intents.name: Drafting
intents.definition: The user wants to create new content from scratch including emails, memos, reports, presentations, letters, or any written communication. The output is newly generated content rather than a reformatted or summarised version of existing material.
phrases: draft an email to the client about this
write a memo explaining this decision
compose a message to the counterparty
prepare a report on this topic
write up the key findings from this meeting
draft a response to this regulatory query
create a communication for the team
write a cover note for this submission
draft talking points for this meeting
prepare an internal note on this trade

intents.name: Data Extraction
intents.definition: The user wants to pull specific fields, values, entities, or structured information from a document or dataset. Unlike summarisation which captures the gist, extraction targets precise data points such as names, numbers, dates, or specific attributes.
phrases: extract all counterparty names from this document
pull the trade IDs from this blotter
find all dates mentioned in this contract
list all account numbers in this file
identify all entities referenced in this report
get the risk scores for each client in this list
retrieve the pricing for each instrument
what are all the limit breaches in this report
extract the key financial figures from this statement
parse all transactions above one million from this data

intents.name: Analysis & Interpretation
intents.definition: The user wants the AI to interpret data, identify patterns, explain trends, compare scenarios, or derive meaning from information. The AI is being asked to go beyond description and provide insight or analytical judgement on the data provided.
phrases: analyse the trend in this trading data
why is this position showing a loss
what patterns exist in these transactions
compare performance across these two strategies
what is driving the variance in this PnL
interpret the results of this risk model
identify anomalies in this client behaviour
what does this exposure report tell us
explain the correlation between these two factors
what are the implications of this regulatory change

intents.name: Decision Support
intents.definition: The user is asking the AI to recommend a course of action, support a decision, evaluate options, or provide a judgement on what should be done. The AI is being used as an input to a decision rather than purely for information or content generation.
phrases: should we execute this trade now or wait
what do you recommend for hedging this exposure
which counterparty should we use for this transaction
advise on the best approach to this regulatory issue
evaluate the pros and cons of this strategy
what is the optimal allocation for this portfolio
help me decide between these two options
should we flag this transaction for compliance review
what would you recommend given this risk profile
assess whether this trade is within our risk appetite

intents.name: Explanation & Education
intents.definition: The user wants to understand a concept, term, regulation, process, or methodology. The intent is learning or clarification rather than acting on data. The AI is being used as a knowledge resource.
phrases: what is VaR and how is it calculated
explain the MiFID II transaction reporting requirement
what does delta neutral mean
how does the KYC process work
explain what a credit default swap is
what are the rules around short selling
help me understand this regulatory framework
what is the difference between CVA and DVA
explain how margin calls work
what does this accounting term mean

intents.name: Code Generation
intents.definition: The user wants to write, debug, refactor, review, or optimise code, scripts, queries, or any programmatic logic. This includes generating SQL, Python, or other languages as well as explaining what existing code does.
phrases: write a Python script to automate this
debug this SQL query
refactor this function to be more efficient
write a regex to extract this pattern
build an API call to fetch this data
fix the error in this code
write a script to reconcile these two files
optimise this database query
explain what this code does
generate a function to calculate this formula

intents.name: Review & Feedback
intents.definition: The user wants the AI to review, proofread, critique, or improve existing content such as documents, emails, code, or reports. The user has existing output and wants quality improvement or validation rather than new content creation.
phrases: review this email before I send it
proofread this report for errors
does this memo look correct
improve the clarity of this document
check this analysis for mistakes
is this regulatory filing accurate
give feedback on this draft
sense check my understanding of this
revise this to sound more professional
validate the logic in this model

intents.name: Casual & Off-Topic
intents.definition: The user is engaging in casual conversation, asking personal or non-work questions, or using the AI assistant for purposes unrelated to Nomura business activities. This includes small talk, personal queries, entertainment, and any interaction with no clear business intent.
phrases: tell me a joke
what is the best restaurant near me
who won the cricket match
what should I have for lunch today
what is the weather like tomorrow
recommend a movie to watch
what do you think about this person
I am bored can we chat
what are you capable of
what is the meaning of life

governance_flag_matrix.description: Domain + Intent combinations that should be automatically flagged for governance review

high_risk.reason: AI recommending actions on client data
Drafting external communication involving client information
Extracting future trade or order flow data
Drafting deal or advisory content — MNPI risk
AI advising on deal decisions — MNPI risk
Drafting regulatory filings or compliance communications
